summary.name: Positive Earnings Surprise Drift Long-only
summary.author: Xin Wei

summary.description_full:
The strategy is a US equities long only factor portfolio designed to capture the post earnings announcement drift associated with positive earnings surprises. It measures standardized unexpected earnings by taking the most recent quarterly EPS and subtracting the EPS from four quarters prior, then standardizing that year over year EPS change by the historical volatility of comparable year over year EPS changes computed over an approximately three year lookback. The intent is to identify firms with unusually strong fundamental momentum relative to their own earnings history, consistent with documented underreaction to earnings information.

The investable universe is the 1000 most liquid US stocks by dollar volume, subject to a minimum price threshold and availability of quarterly EPS data. At the start of each month, the portfolio ranks this universe by standardized earnings surprise and buys only the top 5 percent, forming an equal weighted long book across the selected names. Positions are fully refreshed at each monthly rebalance (i.e., names can be added or removed based on the latest ranking). Risk controls are implicit rather than explicit: liquidity and price screens, equal weighting to limit concentration, and a systematic monthly turnover schedule using daily fundamental inputs.
